title: Are airlines using the war to price gouge on alternative flight routes? | The Business | ABC NEWS
description: The first Australians to leave the Middle East on a commercial flight since the war with Iran broke out are set to touch down in Sydney on Wednesday night.  But with tens of thousands of Australian travellers thought to be wanting a ticket out of the conflict zone, it is going to take some time.  Flight Centre's chief executive Graham Turner says the increase in demand for flights on Asia Europe routes that bypass the Gulf means people will likely be paying more for "at least the next few days, maybe a week". He says Australia to Europe economy flights that cost between $2,000-$3,000 a week or two ago, are now costing around $5,000. When asked if he thinks the airlines are taking advantage of the fact that there is a pool of people right now who feel as though they have no choice but to pay whatever it costs to get on board, he says "you could argue that. But, you know, in the end, the demand is greater than the supply." #ABCBusiness Subscribe: http://ab.co/1svxLVE   ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: Home insurance premiums have risen by more than 50% in 5 years | ABC NEWS
description: Home insurance premiums have increased on average by 51 per cent in just five years, according to data analytics firm Finity.   Homes at risk of natural disasters have the biggest premiums. Residents in Brisbane's west pay the most, averaging $8,396 a year, while homeowners in Darwin city and Sydney's outer west and Blue Mountains region average $5,410 and $5,350 respectively.    Comparing capital cities generally, Finity data shows Darwin's residents pay the most on average for home insurance, followed by Sydney and Brisbane. Finity principal Stephen Lau said an increase in disasters caused by climate change and an increase in building costs had been the main drivers for insurance price hikes.  The Resilience Building Council is working with homeowners to create safer builds, which could reduce risk ratings and lower insurance costs.    Adelaide Miller talks to one couple, who managed to reduce their premium by $500.   ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: Iranian navy ship sinking off Sri Lanka | The World | ABC NEWS
description: ​A top Sri Lankan official says 32 people have been rescued from a sinking Iranian naval ship off Sri Lanka’s southern coast and have been admitted to a hospital.  Dr. Anil Jasinghe, a top health ministry official, says one of them is in critical condition, seven are receiving emergency treatment and others are treated for minor injuries.  Foreign Minister Vijitha Herath told Parliament that Sri Lanka’s navy received information that the ship was under distress, and that the island nation sent ships and air force planes on a rescue mission. South Asia correspondent Meghan Bali spoke to The World's Girish Sawlani about the latest developments.
